title: Aharoni's Rainbow Generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist Conjecture (Conjecture 1.2)
statement_text: Let $n, k$ be positive integers, and let $G$ be a simple graph on $n$ vertices. Let $c : E(G) \to \{1, \ldots, n\}$ be a coloring of the edges of $G$ with color classes of size at least $k$; then $G$ has a rainbow cycle of length at most $\lceil n/k \rceil$.
context_text: Aharoni, Devos, and Holzman proposed this conjecture in 2019 as a rainbow/edge-colored generalization of the classical Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture. Devos et al. had previously confirmed the $k=2$ case. This paper proves the conjecture under the stronger hypothesis that each color class has size at least $301k \log k$.
paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 1.2 in the case where every color class has size at least $301k \log k$, i.e., when the color class size is $\Omega(k \log k)$.
paper_title: On Aharoni's rainbow generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture
